original:
You move in closer to the white woman. Even though you’re only a few feet away, she still hasn’t spotted you, the ambient noise masking your approach. It’s not until you’re standing right next to her does she turn to face you.
	
	Tessa’s eyes go wide, like a kid with her hand caught in the cookie jar, then quickly flutter back into a more natural state. Clearing her throat, she speaks so low you can hardly hear her over the din. "Are you fucking serious? What the fuck are you doing here?" 
	
	Slightly taken aback, you examine her a bit more closely. Her body language is closed off, almost like she’s trying to shy away. You can tell she wants you to leave.
	
	"This asshole bothering you, babe?" An unfamiliar voice hisses, quite literally, over your shoulder.
	
	Turning slightly, you match the voice to a new face. A tall, red-scaled slyveren is standing just behind you, scowling. Running your eyes up and down her impressive figure, you’re not sure whether to try and take a guess at her height, or stare at the massive set of tits she’s got hanging off her. Instead, your eyes hone in on what she’s holding in her hands: two glasses of beer.
	
	"It’s fine, Ahsh." Tessa cuts in, her voice betraying nothing. "This is... [pc.name]. An... acquaintance of mine."
	
	"Hm," the tall slyveren’s face softens, although you can still tell she’s not exactly impressed. Her eyes scan you up and down, perhaps sizing you up, or maybe just taking her first real look at you. Glancing over at the smaller woman, she cracks a half smile. "Let me guess: You two’re fucking."
	
	Tessa winces, which elicits a small snort from the red-scaled woman. "Well, this is awkward." She moves to the seat across from Tessa, swinging her heavy tail out as she sits. "Why don’t you join us... [pc.name], was it?" She slides one of the two glasses across to Tessa, whose jaw is twitching noticeably. "Come on, take a load off. I insist."
	
	"Ahsh..." Tessa starts, her voice laced with both annoyance and concern. "But... the thing."
	
	"It’s fine," the taller woman leans back in her chair slightly. "We’ve got to wait for Tinhead anyways. For now I want to get to know more about the kind of company you keep."
	
	Tessa scoops up the glass and immediately brings it to her lips, appearing as if she’s trying to crawl into it and escape. Over the rim, her bright red eyes motion down to the seat beside you. 
	
	The tall slyveren smiles wide, then motions for you to sit. "Don’t be shy."

You move in closer and [pc.isTaur
		|squat next to the table
		|take a seat
	], positioned with one of the two ladies on either side of you.
	
	"I’m Ahsha, as I’m sure you heard." The tall woman runs her finger tip along the rim of her glass, staring at you intently.

You tell the statuesque woman that you really need to go.
	
	"Aww," she pouts slightly. "Are you sure you don’t want to join us?" her eyes narrow, a playful smile creeping across her lips. "I promise I’m not boring."
	
	Focused on you, she doesn’t notice Tessa shrug, then she nods towards the seat next to you.

Politely refusing, you move away from the table and bid the two women a quick farewell.

	The woman frowns, clearly not impressed with your decision.
	
	Tessa’s reaction is harder to read; the white woman’s face an odd marriage between a smile and a wince.
	
	As you move further away, you glance over and see the two of them talking, the conversation suddenly looking a bit more heated.

You say that you’re fine and don’t need a drink. Ahsha’s lips flatten, her gaze moving slowly between you and Tessa. “Reclamation?” You ask, wondering what she means. “Of the station.” Tessa answers, letting out an annoyed sigh. “The old fucker’s family wants it back.” “Despite its... dubious origins and use, the station is technically their property.” Ahsha shrugs. “I mean, you’d think it being used as an illegal sex haven for degenerate rich assholes would sort of compromise that, but alas...” Cutting her off, Tessa groans. “Without it, they’re fucked. They don’t own anything else, and most of their money was either tied up in the station, or is being used to prop up their fucking insane lifestyles. They want it back, and they’re willing to pay, but they’re that bullshit sort of ‘rich person broke’, where they don’t have any liquid credits.” Clearing her throat, Ahsha nods. “It’s still a tidy sum, but not the sort of money you’d expect from such an... established family.” “So, let me get this straight...” You take a moment then recap the job. “The job is to take back control of the station, and give it back to its rightful owners?” “Yeah,” Tessa clucks her tongue. “Or we could, yanno, not...” Frowning, Ahsha ignores her and looks at you. “We have no idea how much trouble it would be to wrestle back control of the station. I’d mark this down as a ‘maybe’ objective-wise, but since we’re planning on decapitating the leadership anyways...” she shrugs. “Why not?”

“Rescue?” You ask, wondering who’s in trouble. “Ahh,” Ahsha’s eyes gleam. “A very interesting situation...” The pale woman lets out a huff and cuts to the chase. “Some fucking princess went missing.” “Princess?” You do admit that does sound interesting... “No, no.” Ahsha swats at the air, dismissing the notion. “An heiress. A socialite, really. Princess is such a...” she sighs. “Misleading title.” Rolling her eyes, Tessa clarifies. “Some rich girl, Chiaki Hinamori, went to the station and didn’t come back. Went with a few of her ‘socialite’ friends who didn’t notice she was gone until after they were home and sober.” “Her father assumes it was a kidnapping.” Ahsha smiles, her grin so wide she could bite your head off. “And is willing to pay very handsomely for her safe return.” “Assumes?” Something about that word sits in your stomach like a rock. “No ransom. No nothing.” Tessa leans back, her expression betraying her disinterest. “She’s likely so hopped up on stims that she’s still partying, or ran off with some rich assfucker with a fat dick.” “Her father is quite convinced she is still on the station.” Ahsha shifts in her seat, tossing Tessa a glare. “He has considerable resources, and has exhausted all other avenues of inquiry.” “Avenues of inquiry?” Tessa gives Ahsha an incredulous look. “What, you got a word of the day calendar or something going on?” While the two scowl at each other, you consider the job. If the father is convinced she’s there, maybe she is. It could be worth a look. Curious, you ask Ahsha more about the ‘princess’. “Socialite. Well, she’s terran, ‘asian’ apparently, whatever that means. Young. Nineteen, maybe twenty now...” Ahsha strokes her chin, recalling the details. “Short, but I’m not an expert on female terran standards. Quite a beauty I’m told... She went missing a few weeks ago. In fact, it was her disappearance that sparked the tip that provided the lead we have to get aboa-” With a roll of her eyes and groan of exasperation, Tessa butts in. “Her name’s Chiaki and she’s a fucking princess. I think she’ll stick out.” Ahsha mutters ‘socialite’ under her breath for good measure.

“Emancipation?” You assume they mean the slaves. “Yeah,” Tessa crosses her arms over her full chest, her expression serious. “The Reverie is packed with slaves. An anti-slavery non-profit has collected a reward purse for anybody who can help liberate them.” Ahsha slowly blinks a few times, her lips a flat line. “Current management seems to have plucked their stock from the wrong places, in the wrong ways. Otherwise, they wouldn’t have garnered this sort of response. The purse is a decent sum, but not worth the time or effort.” She huffs, frustration obvious. “This really is a waste of time. I only brought it up because Tessa here dragged out her soapbox about it earlier.” You can see the vein in Tessa’s forehead bulge. “Seriously?” Ahsha frowns. “You DO realize where we are, right?” She motions around the room with dramatic flair. “You know what I do here. You’ve always known. Didn’t stop you from letting me fuck you.” She pauses, her nostrils flaring. “Just look the other way like you always do, or stop acting like you don’t know how the galaxy works, babe.” The vein starts noticeably throbbing, Tessa looking like she might throttle the larger woman. At the risk of putting yourself in between them, you ask Ahsha why it’s a waste of time. Sighing, she gives you a tired frown. “Before we could, we’d have to kill every hand holding a chain.” She glances at Tessa, speaking to her just as much as you. “Rather than sneak in, sneak out, we’d have to declare open war. I don’t think the three of us can take down a whole army.” Tessa glares a hole through Ahsha’s head, the vein still visible, but less pronounced. “We should keep an open mind. Maybe it doesn’t need to go down that way.” She turns to you, her eyes softer. “It’s the right thing to try, regardless of pay.” Ahsha turns away from Tessa, giving you an unimpressed look.

“Blackmail?” You wonder who the hell you could be trying to blackmail. “Apparently old man Sears,” Tessa swirls her drink, looking up at the ceiling, “secretly kept records of all his guests.” “And all they did.” Ahsha smiles, her expression absolutely devious. “Ah...” You quickly see where this is going. Tessa salutes you with her mug, toasting your ability to follow. “New management let slip that it exists, and now some very powerful people are panicking. I guess even in this century, people don’t want their darkest kinks getting out.” “They want it scrubbed.” Ahsha explains, her smile still wide. “And are willing to pay a very generous sum for that to happen. But... if that blackmail were to be copied...” Her eyes gleam, her expression shifting from devious to something more akin to hunger. “Well, the potential gains would be far more interesting.” Tessa frowns, obviously less enthused about the prospect. “I don’t give a shit either way. Deleting it would be quick cash, and blackmail is tricky business at the best of times. But, she’s right, the pay difference would be sizable.” “It’s my main objective.” Ahsha proclaims. She gives you a look, her eyes gleaming. “Everything else is secondary: the data is what I’m after.” She gives Tessa a glance and a shrug. “You do you, babe. I’ll do me.” Tessa’s alabaster face is unreadable, her expression guarded, but she gives Ahsha a small nod all the same.

Assassination. This is what you’d expected, given Tessa’s occupation. Still, you ask for more details. Tessa shrugs. “Contract is for the leader of the group that took possession of the station.” A pause. You look between the two women, expecting a better answer than that. Tessa looks serious. Not mad, just serious. Ahsha, on the other hand, is smiling ear to ear. Sensing your thoughts, Tessa sighs and continues. “Private contract. Not open.” She gives Ahsha a glance, frowning at her smile. “Information on the target is limited, both because they haven’t left the station in years, and before they weren’t a big enough fish to matter.” Following along, you nod, but the last thing she said sticks with you. “And why do they matter now?” “They...” Ahsha thinks for a moment, measuring her words. “Pissed off the wrong people. Taking control of the station was a bold move, and not offensive on its own, but the way they’ve conducted their business... Well that earned them the sort of underworld attention few walk away from.” Tessa shrugs. “That’s about the long and short of it. I’ll be taking care of this job personally. You guys don’t need to get involved.” Her words are so cold a shiver runs down your spine.

“So,” Ahsha sneers, “You in?” You look back and forth between the two women, thinking about it. Ahsha, despite her earlier attitude, seems to want you to say yes. Tessa, on the other hand, is harder to read. She DID suggest it in the first place, and she really didn’t have to. She must want you to join, and she must also have a reason. If you say no, that’s the end of it. You’ll get up and walk away, washing your hands of the whole thing. But if you say yes... Something tells you this could be an interesting job. “[pc.Name]?” Tessa’s voice pulls your gaze, her red eyes bedroom soft as she looks at you. “Are you in?”

original: “I’m in.” You declare, deciding to join them. “Excellent.” Ahsha smiles, her teeth bared. “We leave shortly. Our window to board is narrow. I’d suggest getting yourself ready with what little time we have. I don’t doubt we’ll be in for a fight.” “Meet us in the hangar when you’re put together,” Tessa adds, swirling her drink. Downing it, she lowers her glass and swallows hard. “We’re taking my ship. We’ll fill you in on the rest of what we’ve got en route.” She stands and motions for Ahsha to follow. The red woman does, but tosses you a curious look as she stands. “See you soon.” The two women stalk off, leaving you alone. You’re not sure exactly what you’re in for, but you’d better take the time to prepare.

“I’m out,” you answer, deciding not to join them. Ahsha scowls, clearly not happy with your answer. “Seriously?” She turns to Tessa. “What a fucking pussy.” Frowning, clearly not happy with your choice, Tessa offers Ahsha a sigh. “It is what it is. Thanks anyways, Steele. Do you mind?” She motions for you to leave. “Me and Ahsha have to-” “We have to fucking find somebody with a pair of balls, is what we have to do!” As the two start to bicker, you rise up and excuse yourself, Tessa giving you a farewell nod and Ahsha a farewell finger. You’re not sure you made the right call, but this is the bed you’ve made.

As you move towards her, Ahsha notices your approach, stuffing away her file. “You’re late,” she playfully smirks, seemingly untroubled by your alleged tardiness. “But we’ve got time still. Follow me, Tessa’s parked over here.” Following, the two of you make your way through the hangar. While looking around, trying to spot Tessa’s ship, your eyes can’t help but fall on the shapely figure of the woman in front of you. Slyverens are typically curvy, and Ahsha is definitely no exception. Her walk is more of a sashay, her hips and ass swaying side to side with each step, highlighting her pronounced, hourglass figure. Trying not to stare, but failing miserably, you snap to attention when she grinds to a halt, almost causing you to bump into her shapely rear. “Here,” she points to Tessa’s ship, the sleek, black vessel tucked away in a dark corner. “Tessa’s waiting. And I think we both know how irritable she gets when she’s left waiting.” Ahsha lets out a hissing laugh, leading you up the ramp and onto the waiting ship. While still messy, Tessa’s ship has been tidied since you were last on board, with nary a beer can to be seen. You start to ponder why Tessa would clean up (marginally) when she’s usually fine with the place being cluttered. Before you can think on it too hard, you hear Tessa’s voice yell from the cockpit. “You asshats are late. I’m setting a course now. We’ll brief [pc.name] on the way there.” Before you can sit down, the ship comes alive, rumbling as the engines ignite. You sway, staggering as the ship starts to shake, only for Ahsha to brace you, a firm hand on your shoulder. Looking back at her, she gives you a sly grin, her eyes gleaming. You thank her, slipping from her grip, and take a seat, the ship’s shaking making it hard to be upright. Ahsha pulls up a seat across from you, crossing her legs and reclining, all while never letting her stare break. Slightly unnerved, you wait out the rough liftoff, sitting in silence with the red woman. A few minutes later the ship settles, and Tessa stalks into the room from the cockpit. She tosses a look at you and then Ahsha in turn, her lips pressed into a fine line. “Sorry about that. Stabilizers are fucked.” She sighs, moving towards a vacant seat. “Did you start without me?” “No,” Ahsha gives you a coy smile. “Just enjoying each other’s company.” “Right...” if skepticism was tangible, Tessa was oozing it from every pore. Sitting now, she brushes her hair back, running her fingers through that snow white mane. “Got a bit before we arrive. Should we start?”

“Alright,” Ahsha uncrosses her legs, leaning in. “Me and Tess have already talked this over, so this is mostly just for your benefit.” Leaning in yourself, you nod, figuring as much. Ahsha continues. “The Reverie is going to be in a fixed location for ten hours, then it’s going to jump to some somewhere. During that ten hour period, guests are going to be boarding and departing. We’ll be crashing during that turnover.” “Most of those ten hours are already up.” Tessa adds, clicking her tongue. “We were hoping to try and board during the initial shuffle, but...” she motions towards you, “Obviously, our change of plans has compromised a few things.” The way she emphasized the words ‘a few things’ makes you think you’re going to hear about more than a few revisions to the plan. “Either way, based on intel, there is no guest list or check in.” Ahsha scratches her cheek, pushing forward with the explanation. “So long as you have the coordinates, you’re in.” “There is a weapons check, however.” Tessa frowns, her fingers flexing, as if begging to hold one of dangerous tools strapped to her hips. “First order of business is to find a way to bypass it. Or arm ourselves on the other side of it.” Looking at your [pc.weaponMain], you like the idea of bypassing the checkpoint more. Using unfamiliar weapons sounds like a bad idea. “We’ll figure that one out. You,” she points at you with a snap of her finger, “are going to try and find a terminal connected to their network. Any interface will do, really. The goal is to scout out information. Floor plans. Guard movements. Crew quarters. Anything.” “Okay,” you nod. [pc.clMulti engineer |The plan makes sense, given your area of expertise. |While it’s not your area of expertise, it sounds easy enough. ] “After that, we need to get into the VIP section.” Ahsha continues, dropping her finger. “Unlike the rest of the place, there is a list for that, so we can’t waltz in.” “Why do we need to get in there?” you ask, wondering how it ties into the objectives. Tessa answers. “We think that might be where the princess is.” “Socialite.” “Fuck off. Even if she isn’t, an exclusive area like that might yield us an advantage.” “Any staff-only area really does.” Ahsha leans back slightly. “Keep an eye out for offices. That’s a likely place to find the blackmail. Or...” she glances at Tessa. “Certain individuals.” Ignoring the red woman, Tessa keeps her eyes on you. “The bridge is what we really want to find. We’d be able to stop the station dead in its tracks from there.” Ahsha nods. “We’ll have to improvise a bit, to be sure. The main idea is: we board as if we’re normal guests. After splitting up, you’ll progress throughout the general area and try to find a terminal to scout ahead. While you do that, we’ll figure out how to bypass the weapons check and access the VIP area.” “Does that make sense?” Tessa tilts her head.
